name: Tracey Smith
most_valuable: Tips on different  tools to incorporate during lessons. Learning more about teaching special needs.
transportation_detail: Pick up from the overflow parking very quick & efficient
food_detail:
I was only there for the day & only had lunch & snacks. I got to the buffet a little late & there was barely anything left. 
There seemed to be plenty of snack although not altogether healthy. Never did find out where the bottled water & drinks were. For the people only there for a day I think that should've been explained upon arrival.
community_ideas: Maybe do an introductory of instructors attending for the day before the first session is about to begin. I felt those of us that only came for a day or two had to navigate in introductions. Not sure how that would work for someone that's ultra shy..
day_flow_detail: I felt the last session went a bit too long & also the Kaizen kids presentation. Would have preferred to spend more time on the adaptive aquatics.
no_brainer:
Location, pricing & content. 
I wouldn't want to share a room let alone a bed!!
belief_shift: How to teach specials needs, breaking it down. Simplifying steps & skills.
differentiation: I think the camaraderie. It was definitely a lively crowd.
suited_for: Any instructor trying to grow & learn.
anything_else: Better Wi-Fi access. Some of the videos the presenters had couldn't be shown due to connection issues. Also the acoustics were sketchy by the pool. Could be my age & my hearing not being the best, but I couldn't hear much of what Becca had to say.

name: Danielle Jacobson
most_valuable: So much time with so many other swim professionals!
valuable_session: They all were so amazing. The variety of ages, techniques, experience made it so helpful and I was able to learn so much
anything_else: You guys are all amazing! The experience was unmatched and I would love to help with planning the next one :)

name: Shalene Hill
most_valuable: Connection time with other swim instructors
food_detail:
We had such a beautiful indoor and outdoor kitchen, wondering if the catered can cook on site - 
What if there is a cook/caterer/chef in the community, someone related to a swim instructor, who could be in charge of running the kitchen.
They wouldn’t have to cook all the meals, they can make cooking/prep assignments and dish/clean up assignments.
They could come on work passes for thrmself and thier swim instructor…
valuable_session:
Sarah King - working with adults 
Her knowledge and insight are also very helpful for children
future_topics: I want to incorporate group singing throughout each day! Maybe with meals or as an end of day wrap up. The singing was so powerful!
community_ideas: The leadership team was working so hard, especially Kaz… wondering if there is a way to lighten that load.
belief_shift:
I like to think that I am teaching from and AND kind of place and not an OR kind of place:
Gentleness AND pushing boundaries
Nervous System Regulation AND survival skills
Playfulness AND effectiveness
differentiation:
The lasting connections
The opportunity to bring singing and ring in the way that I do.
suited_for:
Any swim instructor at any point on the journey 
It could potentially begin to include partners of swim I  who have a skill to add, like a chef or caterer
anything_else: Oh! I want everyone to have the opportunity to float next time!

name: Kristine Lee
most_valuable:
Staying at the house and being able to have conversations with instructors in a no pressure, comfy setting. Speakers and wrkshops were excellent - not overwhelming. 

I didn’t feel tired at the end of the day unlike other industry conferences. All the info was just enough so that we could recap each session from the day. 

Great planning of breaks in between.
food_detail: Fruits, cheese and cracker snacks. Thanks for the candy! I destroyed the m&m’s.
valuable_session:
We def got value out of all instructor presentations. 

Loved that the sessions were long enough that there was enough question and answer time.
unclear_topic: No. But I loved that if we had question we can easily speak with them in the house.
future_topics: Survival swim and diff variations of teachings, strategies, progressions and troubleshoots.
community_ideas: I think it’s pretty darn strong. The community was built outside the group and coming together strengthened the community!!!
hands_on_detail:
Don’t know how doable it is, if there was a mentor in the water-that would be awesome. Just like the breakout sesh. 

I’m sure there was super value in the breakout sesh that we missed with Becca and Jacob.  So I guess something like that. 

Next time we won’t leave on Sunday. LOL!
belief_shift:
Natalie brought the non physical side. 

Exposure to survival swim, underwater prob solving etc. 

There’s more to aquatic instruction than just strokes. Challenge the mind, fun and mindset.
differentiation: The community was welcoming, everyone cheered for each other, not info overload. The social aspect felt like a hangout rather than a stuffy retreat.
suited_for: Anyone that has their own swim biz that’s not one of the big boxers. Anyone who is growing their biz and making it better.
anything_else:
Thank you from the bottom of our hearts! (Marcus and Kristine). Truly one of the best experiences we had in our lives. 

Jacob and the team: the heart and intention you put into this was evident by how well the event turned out, the type of instructors the community attracts, and the overall energy we feel a part of. SHS is truly something special and we feel so blessed to have come across you guys.

name: Josie Howdyshell
promotion_detail: Posting in the Facebook groups like aquatic managers, swim instructor, and swim school owners.
most_valuable: The talks and small table mentors
valuable_session: Sean’s underwater class was super valuable. It’s going to help me work on retaining older kids who already swim longer
future_topics: How to build my social media
connected_why: I feel like I left with life long friends
day_flow_detail: I have been to many events like this and it has been our favorite. It was well planned. Great flow and lots of topics to meet the needs of everyone .
no_brainer: It’s a no brainer for us as long as it’s not in a peak season
belief_shift: I loved all I learned about adaptive lessons
differentiation: More hands on and smaller group sizes make for  more intimate experience.
suited_for: Swim school owners and people wanting to own a swim school
anything_else: The team is amazing and your dedication does not go unnoticed .

name: Tiana Gomez
valuable_session:
Danielle's method of building up to a  skill with pictures.I laminated, and implemented the cards she shared on my first lesson back home and got results with a challenging screaming student. 

The adult swim session also inspired me to grow into offering adult lessons..

And of course Josie's and David's presentation filled me with bigger picture ideas. 

Too many good sessions to choose just one .
unclear_topic: I think all topics were expanded upon pretty thoroughly
future_topics: Maybe mapping out all different ways to earn and optimize the swim teaching skill. . Such as the aqua fitness instructor, or what Shalene and Natalie did in the pool for water therapy. Might be cool to explore all water aspects outside of swim lessons.
connected_why: I am mostly an introverted person. It dept really nice to let down my guards and experience the warmth and kindness of the instructors who were in the house.
no_brainer: Location... I think Florida was a pretty ideal location. Despite the rain. But it would also be nice in Arizona =)
belief_shift: The five circles of fear.. for adults and children this was very real to understand. Charging what your worth for lessons.
suited_for: People wanting to get into purpose filled work. Other swim instructors or those working in the aquatics industry.
anything_else: This retreat helped me daughter Grace to gain an interest in what I do as a swim instructor. I believe it helped to inspire her to be open to learning more and growing into a swim instructor herself. I am so thankful that we had the opportunity to be there together. It is part of a beautiful core memory and is a foundational building block in who she will become as an adult in the world. Super grateful!

name: Laura Barrios Romero
valuable_session: Having mentors to chat to and the in water hands on approach was great to learn
future_topics: Platform management, client relationships. More logistics.
no_brainer:
1000% location. I’m biased but anywhere in Florida would be the best choice for weather and convenience for me.
Also dates since slower season is easier to book time off
suited_for: People who genuinely love their job and have a eager to grow and get better

name: Jacob Siegel
unclear_topic: No it was all expanded perfectly
future_topics: Business back end, hiring and so on

name: Christine
most_valuable: It’s hard to say what the most valuable part was because each part made everything flow. The one thing I do come back to is how welcomed I felt even before I got off the plane. Megan did an amazing job getting everyone from the plane to the house. It set the tone for what was to come. Again all the people had such amazing information to share. The take away for me was I don’t and shouldn’t teach like everyone else but take what each instructor has to offer and work it in with how I teach best. The balance of classes, mentoring and fellowship was good. There was plenty of time for fellowship and bonding. One of the things I would like to see is during mentoring times have a time limit within each mentorship. I wanted to talk with a couple of them but it was a bit chaotic. When you went to another mentor you had already missed what they had to say.
transportation_detail: Megan was amazing. She made me feel so welcomed even if I am socially awkward.
valuable_session: Asking me to pick one is like asking me to pick a favorite child. I can’t do it. Each one offered valuable information.
unclear_topic: I would have liked to see a demonstration of how the 3,2,1 swim pack is used.
future_topics: How to work with people with disabilities in the water. I retain better with hands on learning.
connected_why: They were easy to connect with. They didn’t act snobby and wanted share the information they have. After they were done with their class they mingled with the rest of us.
community_ideas: I feel like it was done pretty well this time. Having allotted time to mingle is good because it allows us to talk to each other.
hands_on_detail: For Danielle’s class I would love to be able to get into the water and put into practice some of the skills she talked about.
no_brainer: It already is a no brainer for me. I’m going.
belief_shift: Adults and people that are disabled and how to approach teaching them.
differentiation: All they did was lecture. I am a hands on learner.
suited_for: Anyone wanting to teach or are already teaching.
anything_else: I do think having a pool that has different depths would be better suited but it wouldn’t break my heart to go back to the Hollywood walk of fame.

name: Amanda Greenbaum
most_valuable: The all inclusive lodging on site
transportation_detail: I highly suggest keeping the transportation escort it was very helpful and multiple people told me that because they met me at the airport they were more confident talking to me at the retreat . Having someone check in on your arrivals departures was lovely as well!
food_detail: The 3 meals a day as great, more snacks would’ve been great. The quality of the food that the catered provided was not the best it could be
valuable_session: Sean, Sarah, Josie and David, Becca
unclear_topic: How to create a product! Actual steps!
future_topics: More on the money management side of running a business/ Josie
belief_shift: Possibility of a swim school / private business
suited_for: Anyone who teaches swimming, but geared towards business owners

name: Annette Beard
most_valuable: Everyone was was so friendly and willing to share experiences.
differentiation: I've never been to a professional retreat or seminar in the last 20 years.
anything_else: I missed having something sweet after lunch and dinner meals.

name: Megan Wilson
most_valuable: The presentations from each speaker added so much value and got people asking great questions and collaborating
food_detail: There were so many yummy snacks and drinks throughout the day. I don’t think one person was ever not satisfied throughout the day. Electrolytes were a plus !
valuable_session: Building a successful indoor swim school, in the water learning from Becca, and Danielle’s adaptive presentation.
future_topics: Food for thought: We could talk more about building a business like how to calculate rate per lesson depending on location, how to run an efficient mobile swim school, hiring instructors and building a team around your business.
connected_why: The connection with everyone was 10/10. I loved the openness and how everyone was authentically themselves.
community_ideas: With more retreats and people continuing to come back, our community is going to strengthen naturally. Just continue hosting retreats, this was incredible.
day_flow_detail: The screaming on the microphone was a bit much at times but I understand we were trying to stay on time.
hands_on_detail: I loved how you could be in the pool practicing with the baby dolls or sitting pool side and watching closely. Loved the option.
no_brainer: Pricing, reasonable location (close to airports), not too repetitive in presentations.
belief_shift: Learning from Danielle about adaptive swim lessons showed me that remaining creative during lessons and breaking down skills for kids to really understand in their own way is so important. Also just how everyone has their own technique to accomplish the same goal. We all just want children and adults to be safe around the water and to truly love and enjoy the water.
differentiation: The open mindedness and value from this retreat exceeded anything I’ve ever experienced. Retreats like this is what is truly making a difference in the aquatics industry.
suited_for: All swim instructors and business school owners.
anything_else: The retreat team who made this all happen deserve so much. You all made it happen and have really impacted so many lives. Great job.

name: Leigh Ann Miller
valuable_session: Both the infant skills and the underwater problem solving. The extra tips on adaptive swim lessons were also very insightful and helpful.
unclear_topic: No, I only felt probably on Saturday just for a little while that all the different styles were overwhelming and began to question. Should I change my technique altogether. After thinking about it for a few hours, I realized know what I am doing is working. I just needed to take the additional information, the pieces that I loved and integrate that into my own program.
future_topics: I’d love more hands on training with real infants 😆
connected_why: I’m probably one of the older instructors at 53 almost 54 years old. I did connect with several but not all of them. That’s my own fault, though, because the older I get the more introverted I become or at least need my quiet time alone after all the activities.
community_ideas: I don’t really have any suggestions you guys did a great job of having a mix and mingle with each other to get to know each other.
day_flow_detail: The only complaint if I have even a complaint would be after like the last session should’ve been a breakout session where people who were interested in that could’ve gone and listened because that to me was not something that I was interested in and it seemed a bit long and drawn out. The building your own swim business like building pools, the real estate estate, etc..
no_brainer: Location, near the beach, warmer weather.
belief_shift: I just feel like I given More tools in my toolbox to use and great ideas to especially challenge the older kids with the underwater problem-solving and breath control. I love that that’s right up my alley because I love to scuba dive and underwater exploration.
differentiation: Most of the things I attend are just classroom work and studying, which I enjoy learning and having the knowledge of, but I loved interacting with other instructors and hearing ideas and just seeing all of the different ways that you can teach
suited_for: I think it’s most suited for the people that came and they connected with you. Instructors that want to learn and gain new knowledge continue to grow and expand their ideas on teaching lesson.
anything_else: I thought you guys were very well organized, Kaz did an excellent job of keeping everyone on schedule, you greeted everyone with a smile and a hug and made everyone feel welcome, the flow of the sessions was excellent, again just more hands-on time would be the only thing I would like

promotion_detail: I felt like there was on my algorithm. Other people did not. Meaning others in aquatics asked how I knew…
most_valuable: The value for what it was the food that was covered the accommodations what was provided to us.
transportation_detail: For the Publix parking location it would’ve been better if I had a pin or an address drop instead of a photo with the location and address
food_detail: I thought the food was great.  I was never hungry and I was satisfied.
valuable_session: Honestly, the sessions were not as helpful to me as the free time. Being able to interact one on one with people gave me more value. But that’s just my learning style. I’d have to say Becca was my favorite if I had to pick one or Natalie’s.
unclear_topic: No, I felt every topic was extremely clear and well explained. It did not leave me asking any questions.
future_topics: There’s not a topic that I would like to explore more on however, when I took my WSI, there’s a topic assigned and then everybody teaches it so the Takeaway is one person teaches this way. Another person teaches that way I think that level of interaction would be good for our group meaning you can put us in a group of five and we can all show how we do the same skill and then you can rotate us to a new group of five similar to the icebreaker. We did on Friday morning, but with Skills, we all teach.
connected_why: A lot of instructors I felt were engaging and opened a conversation. Some of the instructors I felt were already in their friend group.
community_ideas: I think if we had shorter segments so instead of the instructors teaching for two hours, that’s a long time for me to pay attention if they got to speak for an hour or so and then we as instructors ourselves got to teach and share so I know not everyone wants to teach so you could do on a intake form who’s willing to teach or not or just push people out of their comfort zone also I did a retreat one year and we sat around and we kind of set our goals so kind of like at work you might have a IPA plan. We did this for ourselves for our own individual swim schools at the retreat.
day_flow_detail: I honestly prefer the after hours part better I felt it was more connection and more realness. Maybe if some of that was put into the daytime you know some of the funner activities so instead of being reserved for after hours or before hours, they could’ve been during the day and optional or sign up based.
hands_on_detail: I’m a hands-on learner. I need to do to understand so the seated lecture is not helpful for me but the trying this is doing this
no_brainer: I feel this was an no-brainer, if you add food cost, and stay and travel plus activities and enrichment, discovered all of that
differentiation: The networking, together time. All staying together.
suited_for: Beginning instructors- without a lot of teaching background. At least for the sessions. The rest was more for owners and individual swim school owners to connect.
anything_else: I like that we don’t bring in big names to charge us to get certifications or continuing education credits Were offered at once or the courses were shorter, where we could choose which one we wanted to go to that would put a different spin on it

name: Coach Adam
promotion_detail: Justin because I didn’t know about it until after I asked my social media manager to look into the school group for things I can join
most_valuable: The different speakers with different approaches
valuable_session: Becca’s was for me the most informative because it’s something I don’t know. But ide say the diversity of speakers styles was the most valuable
hands_on_detail: Ode like to see some random swimmers show up  and be taught
belief_shift: For me it was the infants teaching style and how the think about teaching for example teaching the skills backwards is super valuable
differentiation: More personal or community then a big conference
suited_for: I think instructors and a great time for training

name: Darlene Haskins
valuable_session: Games underwater, different ways people teach
connected_why: Met lots of wonderful people and learned a lot from connections made

name: Amanda girton
promotion_detail: I think so. I saw it often in the Skool group. I think yall got lots of content this year to make promotion on socials easier for next year.
most_valuable:
I did a day pass and the space was great. I’m not sure since I was there for one day. The extras may have been worth it baseball court etc 

If those spaces were used too much, then a space with less of those and more rooms, larger pool, more living space may be a better option
transportation_detail: I arranged my own, but having the transport makes the value higher especially for those traveling in
food_detail:
Overall I think it was great. The dinner was meh, not the most flavorful. Catering was a great idea!

I loved that snacks were provided and drinks
valuable_session:
I have already taken special needs courses, but I loved learning some new things from Danielle. 

Becca’s session was great too. ISR is so under wraps, it was great getting some insight into her methods. 

I definitely did not want to hang in the pool to just watch. I think incorporating more hands on would have been good. That would have slightly inticed me to get in. 

In general I felt the sesssions were a bit too long. An hour isn’t quite enough time but 2 hours felt too long. People started to get dazed and leave/distract. 90 mins may have been better. 

Or possibly offer another topic at the same time?

Attending with a day pass certainly is a bit different from the full weekend.
unclear_topic: Not sure since I only attended two sessions.
future_topics: Hmm, not sure what the starting a indoor school was like but in general I’d like more on the business side, scaling, hiring, the admin things people don’t know etc
connected_why: It was hard to meet everyone in a short time, but it was easy to talk to people.
community_ideas:
I knowwwwww you want full weekend attendees, but the day pass option is an excellent offering. 

I think having a QUICK intro of the day passs attendees would be good and intro ing the staff would be helpful so the new people k ow who to look for

Maybe a quick group ice breaker (10mins max). Raise your and if your from the SE, north etc 

However Kaz and Becca did an awesome job welcoming me for the day:)
day_flow_detail:
It was decent. I felt the sessions could have been 90 mins to help stay on track, not lose people and give some wiggle room to not have to rush

I saw the agenda,  adding the group photo would have been helpful. I was in the bathroom and came out to see the group pic happening. 

The lunch mentor sesssions were good. It looked like that was everyday to give everyone a chance to visit each one?
hands_on_detail:
I liked the packets from Danielle!!

Becca’s was great. I think with more baby dolls and a slight bit more organization it could have been more hands on. She did great with the part on the explaining parts too accommodation versus The other words use that I cannot think of right now
no_brainer:
Location date and price. 

Earlier in the year would have likely pushed me to stay at least one night

Location is huge, being in FL definitely sealed the deal on me going. For me and my family and the time of year I wouldn’t have been able to travel to the retreat 

Price is also a huge factor. $2k is a lot of money for mst people. There was a lot of value but sometimes that’s a huge barrier, but I also think that helps get attendees that are serious about their business. 

For me personally I may not attend the full weekend, that’s another story. 

But overall it seems everyone was good with it. 

I think the biggest value is in the connections, those are priceless
belief_shift: Hmm..I love what Becca said which I agree with. We all have sidebar methods but end up in the same place
differentiation: The personal connections. I’ve been to other conferences will left with connections. With this retreat, I left with friends
suited_for:
I think this retreat is great for new instructors or some with a bit of experience. We’re looking to learn a lot of new skills. They may have not learned yet, but I also think it’s great for advanced instructors who, even though they may know a lot of their information looking to learn. New techniques of information they are you know and connecting with like minded professionals who have the same mindset

There are plenty of instructors in my area, but they are not the same level. It’s just their hobby or the teacher on the side and it makes a huge difference connecting with professional professionals who make this their career.
anything_else: I think I’ve covered all of my thoughts in the survey, but if I have more, I’ll message them into you
